steps.title: Identify the metric the question requires
steps.content: The question asks for “the average amount contributed...by each individual employed in wholesale trade.” This signals a per-person metric. The table's rightmost column, “Average contribution per employed person by industry,” directly provides exactly this measurement.

steps.title: Find the intersection of wholesale trade and the average-per-person column
steps.content:
Locate the wholesale trade row and read across to the “Average contribution per employed person” column: \$183,790. The other choices come from different industries' per-person averages (such as \$141,510 for total economic activity) or from employee count columns rather than average-contribution columns (77,247 and 150,373).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
